story.iWant: to automatically detect environmental conditions from sensor data and retrieve environment-specific constraints from the RAG system
story.soThat: I can generate appropriate mission plans adapted to different environments (indoor, outdoor, warehouse, hospital)
story.tasks:
- Task 1: Create EnvironmentDetector Class (AC: #1) - 1.1: Create `src/utils/environment_detector.py` module - 1.2: Define EnvironmentClassification Pydantic model - 1.3: Implement `detect_environment(sensor_data)` method - 1.4: Implement GPS signal strength rule (>0.8 outdoor, <0.3 indoor) - 1.5: Implement Lidar average distance rule (>5m warehouse, <3m indoor) - 1.6: Implement camera brightness rule (natural vs artificial light) - 1.7: Implement confidence scoring and "unknown" fallback - 1.8: Add comprehensive docstrings and type hints
- Task 2: Extend RAG Data with Environment Metadata (AC: #2) - 2.1: Read existing `src/rag/data/environment_constraints.json` - 2.2: Add `environment_type` field to all existing constraints - 2.3: Create indoor-specific constraints (GPS limitation, ceiling detection) - 2.4: Create outdoor-specific constraints (sunlight, weather, GPS reliance) - 2.5: Create warehouse-specific constraints (large spaces, high ceilings) - 2.6: Create hospital-specific constraints (noise limits, smooth motion) - 2.7: Validate JSON schema after modifications - 2.8: Update RAG data loading to handle new metadata field
- Task 3: Integrate EnvironmentDetector with PlannerAgent (AC: #3) - 3.1: Import EnvironmentDetector in `src/agents/planner_agent.py` - 3.2: Initialize EnvironmentDetector in PlannerAgent.__init__() - 3.3: Modify `_retrieve_rag_context()` to call detect_environment() first - 3.4: Extract detected environment_type from EnvironmentClassification - 3.5: Pass `where={"environment_type": env}` filter to ChromaDB query - 3.6: Handle "unknown" environment (fallback to all constraints) - 3.7: Log environment detection result for observability - 3.8: Update method docstring with new behavior
- Task 4: Verify Backward Compatibility (AC: #4) - 4.1: Verify RobotKnowledgeBase class unchanged (read file, check structure) - 4.2: Verify ChromaDB collection list (no new collections created) - 4.3: Test existing RAG queries with new metadata (should work seamlessly) - 4.4: Run Epic 2 RAG tests (pytest tests/test_rag.py) - 4.5: Verify all 14/14 Epic 2.1 tests pass
- Task 5: Unit Tests - EnvironmentDetector (AC: #5) - 5.1: Create `tests/unit/test_environment_detector.py` - 5.2: Test detect_indoor() - GPS=0.2, Lidar=2m → "indoor" - 5.3: Test detect_outdoor() - GPS=0.9, Lidar=10m → "outdoor" - 5.4: Test detect_warehouse() - GPS=0.1, Lidar=8m → "warehouse" - 5.5: Test detect_hospital() - GPS=0.3, Lidar=3m, specific patterns → "hospital" - 5.6: Test detect_unknown() - Conflicting signals → "unknown" - 5.7: Test confidence scoring (0.0-1.0 range) - 5.8: Test edge cases (missing sensors, null values)
- Task 6: Integration Tests - RAG Filtering (AC: #5) - 6.1: Create `tests/integration/test_environment_rag_filtering.py` - 6.2: Test Planner with indoor environment → only indoor constraints retrieved - 6.3: Test Planner with outdoor environment → only outdoor constraints retrieved - 6.4: Test Planner with warehouse environment → only warehouse constraints retrieved - 6.5: Test Planner with unknown environment → all constraints retrieved (fallback) - 6.6: Verify where filter works correctly with ChromaDB - 6.7: Test mission planning produces different results based on environment
- Task 7: Integration Tests - End-to-End Environment-Aware Planning (AC: #5) - 7.1: Create `tests/integration/test_e2e_environment_planning.py` - 7.2: Test E2E: Indoor mission (GPS unavailable, uses Lidar/IMU) - 7.3: Test E2E: Outdoor mission (GPS-based navigation) - 7.4: Test E2E: Warehouse mission (large space, optimized for efficiency) - 7.5: Verify reactive controller + environment detection work together - 7.6: Verify mission completion with environment-appropriate plans
- Task 8: Documentation Updates (AC: #1-5) - 8.1: Update README.md with Environment-Aware Planning section - 8.2: Document EnvironmentDetector API and classification rules - 8.3: Document environment metadata schema - 8.4: Add usage examples (how to add new environments) - 8.5: Update Epic 3 architecture documentation - 8.6: Create environment classification flowchart (optional)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
1. **Rule-Based Environment Detection** - GPS signal strength classifies outdoor (>0.8) vs indoor (<0.3) - Lidar average distance classifies warehouse (>5m) vs indoor (<3m) - Camera brightness differentiates outdoor (natural light) vs indoor (artificial) - Result: "indoor" | "outdoor" | "warehouse" | "hospital" | "unknown" - Test: 4 environment scenarios + unknown case validation
2. **RAG System Extension (Metadata-Only)** - Update `src/rag/data/environment_constraints.json` with `environment_type` field - Add environment-specific constraints (indoor/outdoor/warehouse/hospital) - Example: `{"id": "const_indoor_gps", "environment_type": "indoor", "description": "..."}` - Test: Verify metadata added without creating new collections
3. **Environment-Filtered RAG Queries** - Modify `PlannerAgent._retrieve_rag_context()` to detect environment first - Use ChromaDB `where` filter: `search_constraints(query, where={"environment_type": env})` - Retrieve only environment-relevant constraints - Test: Verify filtered query returns correct constraint subset
4. **Backward Compatibility** - `RobotKnowledgeBase` class remains unchanged - No new ChromaDB collections created - Existing RAG queries continue working (metadata extension only) - Test: Run Epic 2 RAG tests, verify all pass
5. **Comprehensive Testing** - Unit tests: Environment detection logic (4 environments + unknown) - Integration tests: Environment filtering + Planner integration - Test: Planner generates different plans based on detected environment

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Defines environment detection rules (GPS/Lidar/Camera), RAG metadata extension schema, ChromaDB where filter integration, and comprehensive testing requirements. See lines 656-667 for ACs, lines 272-292 for Workflow 3.

doc.title: Epic 3: Advanced Real-time Control & Web Interface
doc.snippet: User story definition, 5 acceptance criteria, files to create (environment_detector.py, test files), files to modify (planner_agent.py, environment_constraints.json). Lines 633-703.

doc.title: LLM_ROBOT_2 System Architecture
doc.snippet: Describes existing RAG system (ChromaDB), Planner Agent architecture, and sensor data flow. Environment detection extends existing patterns without structural changes.

doc.title: Story 2.1: ChromaDB RAG Integration
doc.snippet: Baseline RAG system with RobotKnowledgeBase class, search_constraints() method, and constraint metadata structure. Story 3.3 extends with environment_type field.

doc.title: Story 2.2: Multi-Sensor Integration
doc.snippet: Defines SensorData Pydantic model with GPS, Lidar, and Camera sensors. Environment detection uses these established sensor interfaces.

doc.title: Story 2.0: Test Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Established pytest framework, fixtures, and test organization patterns. Story 3.3 follows same unit/integration test structure.

artifact.reason: This method must be extended to call EnvironmentDetector and apply where filter to ChromaDB queries. Single method modification (~10 lines added).
Existing RAG system with search_constraints() method. Must remain unchanged (backward compatibility). Story 3.3 extends usage with where parameter.
Defines GPS, Lidar, Camera sensor interfaces. EnvironmentDetector.detect_environment() accepts SensorData as input parameter.
Contains existing constraints. Task 2 adds environment_type metadata field to each constraint. New environment-specific constraints will be added.
Manages GPS, Lidar, Camera sensors. EnvironmentDetector extracts features from SensorData provided by this manager.
NEW MODULE - Implements rule-based environment classification. Core logic for Story 3.3 AC #1.
Established pytest fixtures from Epic 1-2. Story 3.3 tests will reuse mock SensorData fixtures.

dependencies.ecosystem:
Data validation - EnvironmentClassification model (Task 1.2) Vector database - RAG system with metadata filtering (Task 2, AC #2) Structured logging - environment detection observability (Task 3.7) <package name="numpy" version=">=1.24.0,<2.0.0">Lidar average calculation, sensor data processing Camera brightness analysis for environment detection (Task 1.6)
Unit and integration testing framework (Task 5-7) Async test support Code coverage measurement (>80% target)
Planner Agent framework (Task 3) RAG system integration
dependencies.external: Robot simulation environment with sensor APIs (GPS, Lidar, Camera)

constraints.constraint: Extends RAG system with metadata filtering only - no structural changes to RobotKnowledgeBase class (AC #4)
EnvironmentDetector must be a utility module (src/utils/) - does not modify core agents directly
PlannerAgent modification minimal - single method update with backward compatible fallback behavior
Environment detection executes once per mission at planning phase - not during execution
Rule-based classification must be lightweight (<10ms latency requirement)
ChromaDB where filter must work with existing metadata structure - no new collections
Must maintain backward compatibility - all 14/14 Epic 2.1 RAG tests must continue passing
Testing follows Epic 1-2 patterns - pytest, unit/integration structure, >80% code coverage target
Confidence scoring required - return "unknown" if confidence <0.5, fallback to all constraints
Must use existing Pydantic models (SensorData) - no new sensor interfaces

interface.name: EnvironmentDetector.detect_environment()
interface.description: Rule-based environment classification. Takes SensorData, returns EnvironmentClassification with type (indoor/outdoor/warehouse/hospital/unknown), confidence (0.0-1.0), and features dict.

interface.name: EnvironmentClassification
interface.signature: class EnvironmentClassification(BaseModel): environment_type: Literal["indoor", "outdoor", "warehouse", "hospital", "unknown"]; confidence: float; features: Dict[str, Any]
interface.description: Output model for environment detection. Includes detected environment type, confidence score (0.0-1.0), and extracted features (GPS signal, Lidar avg, ceiling detected, lighting level).

interface.name: RobotKnowledgeBase.search_constraints()
interface.signature: def search_constraints(self, query: str, where: Optional[Dict] = None, top_k: int = 5) -> List[str]
interface.description: Existing RAG search method. Story 3.3 uses optional 'where' parameter to filter by environment_type. Example: where={"environment_type": "indoor"}. Method interface unchanged.

interface.name: PlannerAgent._retrieve_rag_context()
interface.description: Modified to call EnvironmentDetector first, then pass where filter to search_constraints(). Fallback to unfiltered search if environment=="unknown" or confidence<0.5.

interface.name: SensorData
interface.signature: class SensorData(BaseModel): gps: Optional[GPSData]; lidar: Optional[LidarData]; camera: Optional[CameraData]
interface.description: Existing Pydantic model from Epic 2.2. Contains GPS signal strength, Lidar ranges, Camera brightness. Used as input to EnvironmentDetector.detect_environment().

interface.name: environment_constraints.json schema
interface.description: JSON array of constraint objects. NEW FIELD: environment_type ("indoor" | "outdoor" | "warehouse" | "hospital"). Existing fields (id, description, category) unchanged.

tests.standards: Test framework: pytest (established in Epic 1-2, Story 2.0) Test structure: Unit tests in tests/unit/, Integration tests in tests/integration/ Coverage target: >80% line coverage for new modules (EnvironmentDetector, PlannerAgent modifications) Fixtures: Reuse existing pytest fixtures from tests/conftest.py (mock SensorData, mock RAG responses) Test categories: Unit (environment detection logic), Integration (Planner + RAG filtering), E2E (full mission with environment adaptation) Regression requirement: All 14/14 Epic 2.1 RAG tests must continue passing (AC #4) Performance validation: Environment detection <10ms latency Test execution: pytest tests/unit/test_environment_detector.py tests/integration/test_environment_rag_filtering.py -v --cov
tests.locations: tests/unit/test_environment_detector.py (NEW - Task 5) tests/integration/test_environment_rag_filtering.py (NEW - Task 6) tests/integration/test_e2e_environment_planning.py (NEW - Task 7) tests/test_rag.py (EXISTING - Epic 2.1 regression tests to verify) tests/conftest.py (EXISTING - pytest fixtures for sensor data mocks)

ideas.test: Test detect_indoor(): Mock SensorData with GPS=0.2, Lidar_avg=2m, ceiling=True → assert environment_type=="indoor", confidence>0.8
Test detect_outdoor(): Mock SensorData with GPS=0.9, Lidar_avg=10m, ceiling=False → assert environment_type=="outdoor", confidence>0.8
Test detect_warehouse(): Mock SensorData with GPS=0.1, Lidar_avg=8m, ceiling=True → assert environment_type=="warehouse", confidence>0.8
Test detect_hospital(): Mock SensorData with GPS=0.3, Lidar_avg=3m, specific patterns → assert environment_type=="hospital"
Test detect_unknown(): Mock conflicting SensorData (GPS high, ceiling detected) → assert environment_type=="unknown", confidence<0.5
Test confidence_scoring(): Verify confidence ranges 0.0-1.0, high confidence (>0.8) when rules agree, low (<0.5) for conflicts
Test edge_cases(): Missing GPS, null Lidar, invalid Camera → handle gracefully, return "unknown"
Test PlannerAgent with indoor environment: Mock environment detection → verify only indoor constraints retrieved via where filter
Test PlannerAgent with outdoor environment: Verify only outdoor constraints retrieved
Test PlannerAgent with warehouse environment: Verify only warehouse constraints retrieved
Test PlannerAgent with unknown environment: Verify fallback to all constraints (no where filter applied)
Test ChromaDB where filter correctness: Verify search_constraints(where={"environment_type": "indoor"}) returns correct subset
Test mission planning produces different results: Compare plan for indoor vs outdoor mission → assert different constraints used
Test indoor mission E2E: Full mission with indoor environment → verify GPS not used, Lidar/IMU navigation, mission success
Test outdoor mission E2E: Full mission with outdoor environment → verify GPS-based navigation, mission success
Test warehouse mission E2E: Full mission with warehouse environment → verify large space optimization, higher speed allowed
Test environment detection + reactive controller integration: Verify both systems work together seamlessly
Run Epic 2.1 RAG tests: pytest tests/test_rag.py -v → assert all 14/14 tests pass
Test RAG queries without environment filter: Verify existing usage patterns continue working (no where parameter)
Verify RobotKnowledgeBase class unchanged: Read source file, assert no modifications to class structure
